transcript_text: Welcome everybody to Crystal Kyle with friends. Today we're talking to Josh Citerella of the Doomscroll podcast. Josh, how you doing, man? >> I'm great. Good to see you guys again. >> Good to see you, too. >> Yeah, good to see you, too. Now, you guys all got to go check out uh Doomcroll because he does phenomenal interviews. Uh there was a time when everybody was talking about who's the Joe Rogan of the left and I immediately thought of Josh because he does these long form interviews where he talks to mostly just people on the left which is awesome to have like our own little space where we dig deep for uh you know different personalities on the left. So everybody definitely go check that out. Uh but we have a lot of stuff to get to today. So right off the bat here this is uh kind of a big deal. I don't know if you guys saw this, but Pablo Reports, who's one of the Midest Touch reporters, he's on Capitol Hill all the time, and he spoke to Alexandria Okasio Cortez and basically asked her like, "Hey, is Trump compromised in the Epstein files, and is that why we're in this Iran war? Is there Epstein blackmail that the Israelis are holding over his head, which is leading him to make all these absolutely disastrous, catastrophic decisions?" And her reaction is quite interesting. Take a look at the video. And on Iran, um, do you feel that Trump being compromised as part of the Epstein files is putting our troops at risk abroad? A lot of our adversaries are using the Epstein files as sort of to create the narrative around the war. >> Yeah. You know, I think there was a a story recently, if I'm not mistaken, about how they believe that the files themselves were compromised by a foreign actor. And anytime you have compromising information that is in the hands of a foreign actor, you have the possibility of manipulation, extortion of public officials. And that is extremely dangerous. And the whole point of having that is to force public officials to make decisions that are not always in the best interest of the United States so that they can kind of escape blackmail. You know, we need to know more about the details of the situation, but ultimately we have seen US service members lose their lives in addition to many innocent Iranians. Um, and >> do you think the FC >> and we don't know and there hasn't been justification as to why. >> And do you think the FC files could make Trump a target for blackmail? Then >> we, you know, we don't know what's been compromised of what hasn't. Pam Bondi needs to release the other 3 million files. We have to remember that uh, Attorney General Bondi is still illegally withholding it. She's in violation of the law. And from what we, you know, from what folks have seen in the Epstein files, Trump is absolutely implicated. And we, and that's just from what's been mentioned, that doesn't even include what's been hidden. And so, if there's a foreign actor out there that has more access to the Epstein files than the United States Congress, then we do not know who is compromised, the extent to which they are compromised, and the extent to which blackmail could play a role. But I suspect the odds of that could be quite high. >> Yeah. So to sum up her three-minute answer, yes. She was she could have just said yes, he's implicated the Epstein files and there's blackmail and that's probably one of the main reasons why he's doing this. Josh, what do you think about that? >> That was I think that was the most based I have ever seen AOC. So I'm uh I'm happy to see that clip. Um three predominant theories I think that are circulating online. A quick reminder that we've only had what 1% of the total Epstein files released. So it is completely insane. And a lot of the apenic kind of schizophrenic conspiracy weaving that people are doing on uh Tik Tok and similar platforms uh is just piecing together this like insane information because they're we're missing huge gaps of it. So Epstein connections one. I think the other theory I'm picking up on is just that Israel is massively influential in our government for a whole bunch of reasons we can discuss. And then the third uh fringe um not sure if I believe it, but the geopolitical competition with China and depriving them of access to oil to slow their economy. Um maybe there's an element of that, but I'm a little skeptical whether uh crashing the American economy is actually going to help us to compete with China. So those those are the three that I'm seeing right now. >> Well, also I mean if that was the goal, they've failed miserably because Iran is still able to export oil to China. And that's right. In fact, today Scott Besson is out there like we're actually going to lift the sanctions on Iranian oil so that it could go out into the global market. So masterful gambit fail on that one. But no, I I originally Josh, we've got all kinds of stuff we want to get uh to with you. But I originally booked you because I saw you talking to Emma Vigeland about the Epstein files. And I could tell you had gone really deep on these connections between Epstein himself and the right-wing media ecosystem and some of the conspiracy frenzies. I mean, the connection with Bannon, that's an obvious place, but you also have him kind of at the scene of the crime with the birth of one of these instrumental boards on 4chan, out of which Gamergate comes, out of which Pizzagate comes, out of which, you know, QAnon is birthed as well. And so I I wanted to to get your view on how influential Epstein himself, but the Epstein class more broadly may have been in creating these various scops in American society and stoking a lot of the right-wing culture wars that even lead us to the place where Trump is president of the United States and in a position to be compromised to take us to war with Iran. >> Yeah. So uh this has been the product of much much discussion online. I should say that before we dive into the uh shall we say like conspiratorial web of the narratives that are emerging, I am working on with a dear friend of mine uh Matt Girtson. He was previously a researcher at data and society think tank in New York. He's currently a PhD candidate uh at the history of computer science at Harvard. and we're working on a uh a study about the emergence of pole and the farright elements with a whole bunch of timestamps and historical citations for how that actually came into existence. So um that is currently is not yet published but it is literally years and years of receipts. Um, and it was, I think, very clearly in hindsight, the targeted attempt by Stormfront, a white nationalist message board, and A3P, another one which has since been discontinued and renamed in various other forms of them infiltrating this board, propagandizing, uh, laying out their strategy on 4chan, on poll, and on their own websites for how to influence all of these people. Because if you go back, you know, like uh Occupy Wall Street era, 4chan was anonymous and they were like, you know, hacking people and uh maybe libertarian but kind of culturally progressive. So there's been a major major shift. Um but that is a that's a forthcoming historical citations. We'll get to the uh the nitty-gritty scholarship in a moment here, but the narrative I was discussing with Emma, I'll give you a kind of download of the actual facts that we have from the mere 1% of the files that we're able to access. But we know for a fact that uh Christopher P, who is uh Moot as he's known online, this is the founder of 4chan, that he met with Jeffrey Epstein, within 24 hours of founding the uh message board named Pole, which is the political message board on 4chan. If you're not familiar with this uh interface, there's many many different channels. There's X is one of my favorites for paranormal activity. There's V for video games. And then pole was this space of quotequote containment where all of the kind of far-right elements that were infiltrating 4chan at that time could collect and basically they would get them off of the other boards because people wanted to discuss video games and ghosts and aliens and [ __ ] like that and like why are these Nazis coming in and flooding our thread? It was really annoying to everybody involved. What gets a little bit suspicious and this is kind of where people begin to weave narratives is that there had been in the past in 2008 and 2011 different attempts to start a news board on 4chan. All of those were discontinued quote Christopher P uh discontinuing new which was the uh predecessor to pull um anybody who used it knows exactly why it was removed. When I re-edited the board last year, I made a note that if if it devolved into Stormfront, I'd remove it. So, this is a kind of constant problem in this unedited, ungatekept space where a lot of these far-right elements are coming in. Poll is founded on October 23rd of 2011. There's an email in the current release of what we have access to in the Epstein files on October 24th where Epstein says, quote, "I liked Moot a lot. I drove him home. he is very bright. >> So, you have the opening of this board um within that 24-hour window. There's been some subsequent reporting from The Verge where uh Christopher P denies any influence uh from from Jeffrey Epstein. I am not entirely inclined uh to believe this, but uh he says that the the it was a chance encounter at a social event. I don't necessarily believe that. I think they were uh intentionally introduced. >> I mean, he has an incentive to, you know, not be like, "Yes, Jeffrey Epstein totally direct." Yeah. And yes, he totally directed the, you know, what we were going to do on the website. Anyway, continue. >> Yeah. The Well, the the narrative that I started to discuss with Emma, what what begins to emerge from this is that clearly there is a problem with certain types of users who are flooding other channels on this website. And if you're trying to uh navigate this uphill battle of uh moderation, what you want to do is have a place where you contain them. This is a uh kind of media theory problem where um how do you push back against certain elements? What they did is they made them their own space so that they could all collect in there and just clear them off of the other boards. However, uh as we've seen where when you direct users to certain places, it often fails to contain the narratives and instead it concentrates the most dedicated people to amplify that uh type of activity um to astronomical degrees. So, what has started to unfold since then, we're veering a little bit out here into the um maybe apenic web of Tik Tok conversations, but you've probably come across this. There's a user on Reddit called Maxwell Hill, which went dark immediately when Gilane Maxwell was imprisoned, who was a moderator on a number, it's called a super mod. really really big uh boards, 14 years of activity, world news, technology, politics, science, Europe, uh our/ celebrities, enormous, enormous boards. So potentially a lot of control over the organic narratives that emerge on social media and you have kind of emerging through this uh the narrativization on Tik Tok that Epstein is kind of manipulating the right-wing media. Gilain is manipulating the progressive media and they're basically upping the ante and kind of stoking these uh the most extreme sentiments to thrust us into the online culture wars that we're in today. uh that is supported to some degree by uh different evidence coming from uh the files that we have access to. But this is I would say a pretty strong narrative that has been emerging in the last few uh weeks that we've had access to these files. >> So I just want to boil this down for people to its purest form. If what you're saying is true, all of right-wing politics, modern right-wing politics is in Epstein Israeli SCOP. >> That would not be entirely inaccurate. So, one of the one of the more my favorite pieces of supporting evidence for this, I'm really of the opinion now that like we should not be holding the gates or like doing the liberal factchecking stuff. I'm very much of the uh uh Kyle's persuasion of like I'm part of the law and order left, right? I think we need to take an acceleration of stance on this and uh really be tough on crime, specifically elite pedophilia, that type of crime. But JF Garrape, do you are you familiar with this [ __ ] guy? Okay. Yeah. this uh [ __ ] Nazi piece of [ __ ] Excuse my language. He used to run a science channel called Neurot TV from which he was given not like gifted from a nonprofit 501c3 or something, but like literally just a check from signed Jeffrey Epstein in his own handwriting for $25,000. So, this was on, you know, he's a donor to MIT and all this stuff. um he's a patron of the sciences but he was JF Garpe was starting a YouTube channel at that time he gets 25k from Jeffrey Epstein and so there are these you know literal factual documented connections that are not just conspiratorial or extrapolating out the relationship with Bannon and Breitbart and things like this there are a few things that actually do connect them >> I mean this is what to me has been so crazy about the Epstein files because like obviously as a leftist it's almost cliche to say like they use the culture war to distract you from the material issues and divide and it's like >> but I had kind of like a more abstract idea of how that might have been unfolding and it's like no he met with the guy who you know on the day that he started RP Paul and he funded this like white sup you know neo-Nazi um creator and obviously has this close relationship with Steve Bannon etc. So, let's actually get in a little bit to the the Steve Bannon piece. Um, what do you make of their relationship? You know, was it all of these relationships are transactional on some level, but like what was the transaction there? And then, if memory serves, didn't Epstein give Bannon advice about like starting a media company and and push him in that direction? >> Uh, I mean, look, I'm a real cinnaphile and I've seen um most, if not all, of Steve Bannon's documentary films. He's a a very accomplished director. Um I think that the my favorite theory of the Bannon Epstein relationship is like the most elaborate cope that you could possibly imagine is that Steve Bannon is deep deep undercover and he is attempting to get Jeffrey Epstein to incriminate himself on camera because he's actually still carrying forward the torch of uh American patriotism or something like that. I think what is I that's complete [ __ ] I think what is clearly evidenced is that Bannon is an operator. A lot of his rhetoric and ideology is um I think a convenient ruse and he's clearly you know he's this is a guy with a lot of money. He can fund his projects. um the the story that people are kind of uh uh building into this because you know um in the history of the internet culture wars Gamergate was such an extraordinary force that um it's uh it's instigated to give a little bit of the history for this because I this is common knowledge to me but maybe not to everyone else. Gamergate begins on 4chan. It is then banned the discussion of Gamergate is banned on 4chan and then there's another website that starts up called 8chan. Um, Achan becomes the home of Gamergate and the home of Q. I think that what these videos show from Bannon is that he is a kind of you know intelligent operator, a shrewd operator and maybe less of an ideologue that uh we have been tempted to credit him with in the past. this is a rich patron and he can get money from him and resources and um maybe was kind of charmed but uh yeah, Emma suggested to link all these things together that Bannon was aware of Epstein's abuse of children and so they through these different message boards and their kind of media empire begin to create uh a diversion that the elite pedophilia is something that has to do with the Democrat party to redirect the allegations from themselves, right? The like factually correct allegations uh of the convicted child molester, Jeffrey Epstein. >> I think there the other possibility too is that I mean obviously it's convenient for them that it's you know the Clintons and Podesta in the Pizzagate conspiracy and that sort of thing. >> But it's also Joe Kent I don't know if you've watched the whole Joe Kent Tucker interview yet, but at one point >> I saw some of it last night. Yeah. At one point he suggests that the government uses a strategy of if there's something that you know they want to keep hidden. So I guess that's right. Same thing with QAnon.
